channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico for a month
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that their planned tariffs were on hold for a month to give time for further negotiations. The statement was confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Salvage crews remove large portion of commercial jet from river after deadly air collision near DC
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — Salvage crews have removed a large portion of a commercial jet from the Potomac River near Washington’s Reagan National Airport on Monday, five days after a midair collision last week that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, MICHAEL R. SISAK and CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Musk is a 'special government employee,' the White House confirms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is working for President Donald Trump as a “special government employee," according to a White House official, solidifying his controversial role in the administration but sidestepping some disclosure rules that are typical of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Here's the breakdown
item.description: Experts say every state gets millions in federal help — and a little more goes to Republican states and congressional districts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KREX 5

item.title: Wall Street swerves lower after Trump announces tariffs and then puts some on hold
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Wall Street swung between sharp and more modest losses on Monday as stock markets worldwide sank on concerns President Donald Trump’s tariffs may ignite a punishing trade war. The S&P 500 was down 0.6% in midday trading after Asian and European indexes logged worse drops.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Britain's Starmer seeks strong ties with both the EU and Donald Trump. That could be tricky
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Keir Starmer travels to Brussels on Monday, becoming the first British prime minister to attend a European Union leaders’ meeting since the U.K. left the bloc five years ago.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump announces pause on Mexico tariffs after striking deal
item.description: Trump revealed he negotiated assistance from Mexico to strengthen the United States' southern border.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Senior officials at the USAID were placed on administrative leave after they refused to turn over classified material.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Minneapolis to pay woman who says Chauvin knelt on her back. Here's how much
item.description: The city of Minneapolis will pay $600,000 to a woman who alleged that former policeman Derek Chauvin pinned her to the ground with his knee in January 2020, just months before he used the same maneuver on George Floyd.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KREX 5

item.title: Lady Gaga defends transgender community during Grammys speech
item.description: "The trans community deserves to be lifted up," Lady Gaga, who was born Stefani Germanotta, said to applause.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Beyoncé announces first cities for Cowboy Carter Tour
item.description: Beyoncé has announced the initial cities for her Cowboy Carter & The Rodeo Chitlin’ Circuit Tour, including Los Angeles, Chicago, New York, London, Paris, Houston, Atlanta, Washington, D.C., and Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration's push for a federal funding freeze is back in court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A planned Trump administration freeze on federal funding is heading back to a Washington courtroom on Monday.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man hit in head by debris from Philadelphia plane crash that broke through diner window
item.description: A Philadelphia diner patron was injured Friday when a piece of debris from a medevac jet that crashed hurtled through a window and hit the man in the head.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American bald eagles are having a moment, ecologically and culturally
item.description: WEST ORANGE, N.J. (AP) — Along the long road from American icon to endangered species and back again, the bald eagle — the national bird of the United States, often seen against a clear blue sky — is having a moment.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Demonstrator steals police car during anti-deportation protest in Arizona
item.description: Demonstrators in Arizona protested against President Donald Trump's mass deportation plan on Sunday, with police saying officers were assaulted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arkansas nurse charged with using dead patient's name in attempt to purchase hundreds of oxycodone pills
item.description: A nurse in Arkansas is facing felony charges after investigators say she used a dead man's name when attempting to purchase oxycodone pills at pharmacies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doncic releases emotional farewell message to Dallas after trade sends him to Lakers
item.description: Luka Doncic released an emotional farewell to Dallas on Sunday, the same day a trade sending him from the Mavericks to the Los Angeles Lakers in a swap for fellow All-NBA player Anthony Davis was finalized.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chase Elliott wins Clash at Bowman Gray and sets sights on following it with Daytona 500 victory
item.description: NASCAR returned to its roots with a fight-free preseason exhibition at Bowman Gray Stadium and a popular victory with Chase Elliott winning The Clash on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor says 5 injured in fatal Philadelphia plane crash remain hospitalized, 3 in critical condition
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Three people injured on the ground when a jet crashed in a busy Philadelphia neighborhood, killing seven people, remain in critical condition, Mayor Cherelle Parker said Sunday. Parker said 22 people were injured and five of them remain hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Luka Doncic heads to the Lakers and Anthony Davis goes to the Mavs in blockbuster trade
item.description: The trade was put together in secret, with only a precious few people knowing what was happening. Luka Doncic didn't know. Anthony Davis didn't know. Their coaches didn't know. Their teammates didn't know. And then it happened — an absolute blockbuster.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of impersonating US Border Patrol agent twice in one week
item.description: A Wisconsin man is being accused of impersonating a U.S. Border Patrol agent twice in January after approaching police officers and claiming to be off duty, according to prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of North Carolina mom missing since 2023 found by dive team: deputies
item.description: Dana Mustian never showed up at her daughter’s 6th birthday party in December 2023. Her remains have since been located by a dive team.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, Fox 5

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Families visit DC crash site days after the deadliest US air disaster in a generation
item.description: Dozens of people walked along the banks of the Potomac River near Reagan National Airport, close to where an American Airlines jet and an Army Black Hawk helicopter collided Wednesday, killing all 67 aboard.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Fans boo US national anthem at NHL game in Canada
item.description: The booing came shortly after Trump signed an order imposing stiff tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Airport employee injured in collision with aircraft wing at Chicago's O'Hare
item.description: The driver of a tug vehicle at Chicago O'Hare International Airport was injured Saturday evening after colliding with the wing of an airplane.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Opinion: Why Trump's meme coin is a cash grab
item.description: The coin – officially called $Trump – briefly ascended into the top 15 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization and attracted over a half-million buyers.
item.dc:creator: Maximilian Brichta Doctoral Student of Communication, University of Southern California, Fox 5

item.title: Air traffic controllers union responds to Trump's DEI claims
item.description: The air traffic controllers union responded Friday to claims by President Trump this week that diversity programs contributed to the fatal crash in Washington Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, ABC 4

item.title: Court says banning gun sales to young adults under 21 is unconstitutional
item.description: BATON ROUGE, Louisiana (AP) — A U.S. appeals court on Thursday ruled against a federal law requiring young adults to be 21 to buy handguns, finding it violated the Second Amendment. The ruling, handed down by a panel of three judges on the conservative U.S. 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WGN 9
